podcasts.name: Daan Jonkman — AI for Shopify
podcasts.title: My Team Asks Slack a Question and Shopify Answers (Claude Agent)
podcasts.transcript: I typed this one question into my Slack channel, "Who is our biggest customer this year?" And my Shopify agent just answered it. Name, total spend, last order, and I didn't touch the admin once. So, in this video, I will show you exactly what this agent can do. And a sneak preview, it can look up sales data, customer lookups, it can create coupon codes, it can update product data, and by the end of this video, you will have this cloud and Shopify agent living inside of your own Slack that your entire team can start using. And the best part, this agent gets more intelligent the more it's being used. And on how to set up a server for this, I have a full guide in the description down below that you can download for free. And here, I will focus on the part that nobody shows you, and that's getting this inside of Slack, connecting it to Shopify, and having this infrastructure behind it that is self-learning. So, stay along, and we will have this running for your own store. I will start by showing you this agent in real time. So, I ask a question, "Pull my top three customers of the last two months. Give me an overview of what they ordered, what the AOV was, and identify if it was a first-time sale or a repeated buyer." And as we see here in the thread, the Shopify agent just started querying at Shopify, got the data, and came back with an answer for me. We see it's a repeated buyer, the total spend, the orders in total, and the AOV. Then I said, "Okay, create me a 10% discount code for only for these customers with a one-time use coupon. Also, add their name in the coupon, identify what they also might like based on what they ordered before, give that suggestion, and also send a short thank you email with the coupon and with the product suggestion." It started creating the discount codes only for these customers. It added the name in the coupon and the expiry date. It added tags to the customer profile. It's a top customer, VIP customer, and it gave product suggestions. I haven't connected my email yet, so it was not able to send these emails directly to the customer, but for now, it is okay. We can also just create a draft and send it manual, or you add this functionality to the agent and also ask the question, "Who is our biggest customer this year?" It gave me the top 10 overview. I say, "Give me all the details of the top customer. Give me the details, email address, address, and the order summary, what she bought." Now, let's ask one more question to the agent. Okay, based on the product, the top product that Femke de Boer ordered, our customer, identify what the product description is, what the SEO meta fields are, and see if we can optimize it even further. And as we see here, the Shopify agent is analyzing it. It's now pulling all the data and coming back with an answer. We see here in the thread. It's still working. Here we can see the full overview of what this agent outputted to us. Top product, current description, really short one. As we see, the meta fields are copy-paste of the product description. So, it was not set. Shopify set this automatically because we haven't added a optimized meta title and meta description yet. So, it suggests the optimized one, and also the title and the optimized product description. Yeah, let's go ahead and implement this, and let's go with the occasional angle for the meta title, and directly push it uh to draft first, and give me the Shopify admin URL so I can check it for myself before we publish it. And you don't need to tag the agent when you're already inside of a thread. Can also set it up that you don't have to tag the agent at all, but I do like it more because, yeah, when you have a general conversation inside of this channel, you might not want the agent to be triggered all the time. Let's open the admin. And as you can see, it already added the description to here, and also the meta fields. And if we're satisfied, we can either do it manual, set it to live, or we can go back to the agent and say, "Okay, it looks good. Let's push it to live." As you can see, it's live now. Let's double-check it, and the product is active now. So, this is a great use case on how you can quickly update a product, or how you can look up data without going to the Shopify admin all the time. So, now that we know what this agent can do, let's dive in on how we can set this up. This is everything that we need to have this up and running for the store. And if you do want my help with this to have this set up for you, you can book in a call in the description. And if you want to do it yourself, we have the free guide in the description on how to set up the virtual private server and install the harness in here. And I use open router to switch between AI models to choose the best model for the task. So, we are also aware of token usage. So, that is in the description. And when you have set it up, we need a Slack app and a Shopify custom app. And in this Shopify custom app, we can set all the permissions, what this agent can and cannot do on your store. So, let's start with the Shopify app. First thing that we do, we go to the Shopify admin and we press on apps here. And if you don't see the apps yet, you go to settings. Press apps and here press develop apps. Build apps in dev dashboard. And here we can create a custom app. Press on create app. You give it a name. You can name this whatever you want. And here we need to scroll to access. And we need to select the scopes, the access scopes what this agent has access to. Press on select scopes. Press on admin API. And here you can see for yourself what you want to give it access to. In my case, um I'm going to give it access to everything. You can also do just read uh only. So, it cannot update things. It can just read your data. And know that you do have to press everything manual, so it can take some time. Once you select everything, you press on done. And then we can press on release. Now, we created the app, but we do have to install it inside of our Shopify store. So, we press on this one. And here we can press on install app. There you give it access to the store that you want to install it in. And finally, you press install here. And then we'll see the app that we just created inside of um the dashboard here. And here inside of settings, when you go back, you will see your client ID and your client secret. And these two keys we need and set up on the virtual private server. And I will show you how you can do that. Here I am inside of cursor. This is a IDE, a code editor, and I have an direct connection with my virtual private server. So, I can tell Claude what to do on that server, so we don't have to go in the terminal inside of the server itself. Okay, I am connected via SSH to my VPS server, the Shopify agent server. I want to add my Shopify client ID and client secret to there. And make sure it has a script in there to get the actual API token that it needs to update things on my store. And this might sound a bit technical. And it kind of is with setting up SSH. We do have the full guide in the description, and you can download that and walk through step-by-step on how you can set this up. And here I will paste in my client ID and client secret. And what this last part means, the API token, it gives us a secret, but it does need to run a script in order to get a actual workable API token. So, I will copy my secret. I will also paste it in here. And be aware what you share with an AI or with other people. I'm going to delete this app afterwards, so it doesn't matter in my case. First, check the connection with the server before you actually implement something. And also this script that it needs to run is also in the full guide in the description. As we see, it is working. They tested if we get that workable token. It has full access. So, then we can go over to the next step, and that is connecting our Slack app. And once we're in api.slack.com, we press on create new app. And then we press on from a manifest. We select the workspace we want to deploy the app in. Press on next. And in the full guide, there's also this piece of code. You can paste it in too. Don't have to set this up all manual. And do note that you can change the name of the uh agent, but now it's set to this one. We press create, and now we only need to do a few things. Now we have to install the app inside our workspace. So, we go to install app, press on install, and then we press allow. And now we get it this bot user token. We copy this, we save this somewhere. Now we also need to get a app token. Then we go to basic information. We scroll down to app level token. We generate a token and access scope. Press on write, read, and this one. And we also copy this token. And once you've copied this token and also the other token, we need to go back to Cloud Code to update this on the server. So, I do have a full uh script here or a prompt here that you can run. The only thing you need to add is your SSH here, your alias, and also the Docker container, what the actual name is of your container, and if you're on Hermes or another one. And here you paste the Slack bot token that we copied in the first step, and then the second step, what we just copied. And then we run it. And again, if you do need help with this, if this gets too technical, you can just book in a call, and me and my co-founder can help you out. Okay, as we see the the credentials are updated now on the server, so we can actually start inviting our bot into the Slack channel. I created a new channel. We'll press {slash} invite, and here we will type in the name of the agent, how we called it. And now we see the agent has joined. So, we can start tagging him and asking questions. Pull the last order I got from my Shopify. As we see, he does the eye icons, so it's on it. We see it's analyzing, and it replied. It gave me back the answer. And we can do the same as you saw in the beginning. So, this is how you set up this agent. And if you do need help with this, feel free to book in a call and me and my co-founder can help you out. Now, let's ask it one more question. Now, pull the product Fins ordered and give me the product description and meta title meta fields and also the pricing. And the nice thing is your whole team can use this inside of this channel or the channels you deploy it in. And you can also create different agents with different access scopes in different channels to keep it even more organized and safe. Came back with the answer. As we see, the description is still very general and short. So, update the description and also add good meta title meta descriptions. And we can optimize this agent even further. You add SEO practices. You add MCP connections with this agent so it can get real-time data. You can make this as powerful as you want, but the key is to have it deployed inside of Slack, connected with Shopify, and have this self-learning system in the back end. Let me show you how this agent looks in the back. I just logged into my Hermes agent, and here we can see all the recent sessions. It takes like 5 minutes to update. So, we can see the questions that we asked. As we saw in the beginning, we asked about the SEO optimization, the top customers, and it stores these sessions. And if there are repeated questions, the agent will learn from this. It will optimize itself. So, this runs in the background. Whenever somebody is chatting with this agent, it improves itself. So, that's the best part about this setup, in my opinion. So, I hope to see you in the next video and hope you got some value out of this. And again, if you do need my help, just book in a call and I will see you there. Bye.
